action.reasoning: Observation: the revert attempt failed because checkpoints/best.pt now contains HybridDLinear weights, while predict.py instantiates plain DLinear, causing state_dict key mismatch. Analysis: the original best DLinear checkpoint was overwritten, so I cannot simply resubmit it. With limited time, the safest recovery is to make predict.py architecture-aware and retrain plain DLinear briefly to recreate a compatible checkpoint, then smoke-test and submit. DLinear previously beat the hybrid and is lightweight enough for a short CPU run. Decision: update train.py to use DLinearForecaster again with robust checkpoint metadata including model_type, update predict.py to instantiate based on model_type with backward compatibility, train a short DLinear run, smoke-test locally, and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 8 --batch_size 128 --lr 0.002 --device cpu && python predict.py --data_path smoke_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output smoke_test/out.json --device cpu
